subject: Physics
title: Splitting the Atom
description: A short lesson to help you apply Splitting the Atom.

hook.text: In 1938, Otto Hahn split a uranium atom and released energy that would change the course of history.

buildup.text: Hahn bombarded uranium with neutrons expecting small changes. Instead, he found barium—half of uranium.

discovery.text: Lise Meitner, exiled from Nazi Germany, explained the physics: the nucleus had split in two—fission.

twist.text: Meitner coined the term 'fission' but was denied the Nobel Prize. Hahn won it alone in 1944.

climax.text: Fission released millions of times more energy per atom than any chemical reaction.

punchline.text: One atom split. The nuclear age began. Credit went to the wrong person.

quiz.question: Who provided the theoretical explanation for nuclear fission?
